purpose: Conformance substrate for the GuardedMemory pattern: action_type='memory_write' + scope=<memory_key> produces a content-addressed receipt per write. Covers OWASP ASI06 gap class #2 (memory provenance attestation). Designed for memory framework integrations (LlamaIndex, AutoGen, LangChain) where each memory write must be independently verifiable without trusting the operator.
owasp_coverage: ASI06 — memory poisoning defense via content-addressed write receipts

vectors.description: Baseline memory write receipt. action_type='memory_write', scope identifies the memory slot. Reproducing this byte-identical satisfies the memory provenance attestation requirement. This vector is byte-identical to the example in docs/spec/action-ref.md (memory provenance section, commit de7dd7e).

invariants.scope_is_memory_key: scope identifies the specific memory slot — different slot = different action_ref. Two writes to the same slot at the same timestamp by the same agent produce the same action_ref (idempotent receipt).
invariants.no_content_in_preimage: The written content itself does not enter the preimage. The receipt proves what slot was written, by whom, at what time — not the content. Content integrity requires a separate commitment (e.g. content hash in claims).

vectors.description: LlamaIndex GuardedMemory write — conversation_history slot. Models the exact pattern a LlamaIndex memory backend would use when anchoring each memory write. scope follows llm:<framework>:<key> convention for cross-framework interoperability.

companion_read.description: The corresponding memory_read receipt 30s later — proves the read was within scope.

invariants.write_read_independence: memory_write and memory_read produce different action_refs even for the same slot — action_type differs, so the hash differs. A verifier can distinguish a write audit trail from a read audit trail.
invariants.scope_convention: llm:<framework>:<key> — namespaced to avoid collisions across frameworks. The convention is advisory; any non-empty string is valid per spec.

vectors.description: GuardedMemory write under orchestrator delegation — tool_results_cache slot. Models a multi-agent scenario: orchestrator-001 delegates memory.write capability to llamaindex-agent-001 for a specific scope and time window. delegation_ref links the authorization chain to the write receipt.

invariants.delegation_ref_not_in_preimage: delegation_ref does not enter action_preimage — action_ref is determined solely by action_type, agent_id, scope, timestamp
invariants.scope_match: scope in delegation_artifact matches scope in action_preimage — the agent is writing only within the authorized boundary
invariants.capability_granularity: memory.write capability is distinct from memory.read — a delegatee with only memory.write cannot produce a valid delegation_ref for a memory_read action

guarded_memory_pattern.summary: Wrap any memory backend's put() method: derive action_ref before writing, anchor the trail after. The receipt is independent of the backend — S3, Redis, SQLite, LlamaIndex SimpleDocumentStore, any storage.
guarded_memory_pattern.python_snippet:
import hashlib, json
from datetime import datetime, timezone

def guarded_put(backend, agent_id, memory_key, content):
    ts = datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime('%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.000Z')
    preimage = {"action_type": "memory_write", "agent_id": agent_id,
                "scope": memory_key, "timestamp": ts}
    action_ref = hashlib.sha256(
        json.dumps(preimage, separators=(',',':'), sort_keys=True).encode()
    ).hexdigest()
    backend.put(memory_key, content)   # your existing write
    anchor_trail(action_ref, preimage) # POST /nexus/trail
    return action_ref
guarded_memory_pattern.scope_convention: Use <namespace>:<framework>:<key> for cross-framework interoperability. Examples: llm:memory:conversation_history, llm:memory:tool_results_cache, mycelium:memory:session_context_v3
